heading: From Bali to Cave Country
paragraphs: Growing up landlocked in the Midwest, the closest I ever got to the ocean was late-night television — losing myself in The Undersea World of Jacques Cousteau and falling in love with a world that felt entirely alien. It would take decades, a move to Huntington Beach, California, and a life-threatening illness before I finally did something about it.

In 2016, I became a certified Open Water Diver through PADI. I was hooked from the first breath underwater. Within a year I'd relocated to the United Arab Emirates with one goal: become an instructor. That dream became real in late 2018, while I was also managing a dive center in the UAE.

From there, the ocean took me to Bali. For four years, I served as Managing Partner at Prana Dive, a boutique full-service dive center in Amed. At Prana I was teaching everyone from first-time Try Dive students to advanced technical divers, and obsessing over the discipline I love most: sidemount diving. I even got to talk shop on the Speaking Sidemount podcast, where host Steve Davis and I went through what it really takes to be good at sidemount, from soup to nuts.

My credentials span multiple agencies and disciplines: I was a PADI Open Water Scuba Instructor, Specialty Instructor, and Emergency First Response / CPR with Care for Children Instructor. Now I am a RAID Crossover Instructor Trainer a technical open circuit instructor, a Normoxic CCR diver on the Poseidon, and a factory instructor on the Trident Chest Mount CCR

On June 1, 2026, I came home. I moved back to the United States to take on the role of Brand and Customer Experience Manager at Deep 6 Expedition Gear, where I get to bring the same obsession with safety, performance, and the diver experience to the equipment that makes deep exploration possible.

I'm also chasing a long-time goal of my own: becoming a full cave diver in the limestone labyrinths of Florida cave country — training in some of the most demanding and rewarding environments diving has to offer.

And outside the water, I've been building something I'm just as passionate about: the AXIS Method, a systems-engineering diagnostic framework I founded on one core principle — when alignment is restored, complexity collapses. It's a way of thinking I apply to diving, teaching, and pretty much every complicated system I run into, and it's become as core to how I work as any C-card I hold.

Whether you're a complete beginner taking your first breath underwater, a tech diver pushing into cave country, or a brand looking to build gear divers can trust their lives to — I'm here for all of it.

Let's go deeper.
— Turk Vangel | Brand & Customer Experience Manager, Deep 6 Expedition Gear | Founder, AXIS Method | Current Cave 1 Diver | RAID XO Instructor Trainer
